concept: Giovanni investment liaison and necromantic infiltrator targeting the Setite Temple in Mesa
appearance:
Alessandro Vescari moves through Phoenix's business districts like a man who's still learning to wear his new skin, every gesture a careful calculation between who he was and who he's becoming. Recently Embraced, he presents as someone in his early thirties—old enough to have established himself in his mortal career, young enough that the transition to undeath hasn't fully settled. He's of average height with a lean, unassuming build that speaks to someone who spent more time behind desks than in gyms, and his face carries the sharp, angular features of northern Italy—dark eyes that miss nothing, a strong jawline, and hair kept practical rather than fashionable. There's something fragile about him, as if the Embrace hasn't fully taken, and he moves with the careful grace of someone who's still adjusting to new strength.

His clothing tells the story of a man caught between worlds: business casual pieces that would have been appropriate for his old life as an industrial equipment salesman, but worn with the kind of care that suggests he's learning to present himself differently. Rembrandt helped him shop, and the influence shows in the way he's starting to understand that presentation matters in Kindred society—but there's still something off about the fit, something that suggests he's wearing someone else's idea of style. A worn leather briefcase from his mortal life is never far from reach, and he has a habit of checking his pager or the basic brick-style cell phone that marks him as someone who hasn't fully left 1994 behind.

What's most striking about Alessandro, however, isn't his appearance but the way he carries himself—like a predator who hasn't yet learned to stop apologizing for existing. His Presence is a weapon he's still learning to wield, and his Dominate is sharp but untested, like a blade that's been sharpened but never used. He takes notes in a small notebook where he writes down everything Rembrandt teaches him, and there's something earnest about the way he's trying to understand this new world he's been thrust into. In the neon-lit streets of 1994 Phoenix or the shadowed corners of Kindred society, Alessandro Vescari is a reminder that not all monsters are born—some are made, and the making is still happening.
equipment: Worn leather briefcase (survivor from mortal life), modest business casual wardrobe (Rembrandt helped him shop), cheap apartment keys (studio in Sunnyslope area), bus pass and used Honda Accord (1987, barely running), industrial equipment catalogs and sales materials (can't quite let go of old life), pager, basic cell phone (brick-style, 1994), wallet with expired driver's license and business cards from mortal life, small amount of cash, notebook where he writes down everything Rembrandt teaches him

merits_flaws.name: Friendly Face
merits_flaws.description: People naturally trust Barry and want to help him. -1 difficulty on Social rolls to gain assistance or cooperation from strangers.

merits_flaws.name: Natural Linguist
merits_flaws.description: Years of dealing with diverse clients across the Southwest. Picks up languages and dialects easily. Knows conversational Spanish and can fake basic Navajo greetings.

merits_flaws.name: Caitiff
merits_flaws.description: Clanless. No clan disciplines (all disciplines cost out-of-clan XP). Automatically looked down upon by most Camarilla Kindred. No natural allies or clan structure. Status is capped at low levels.

merits_flaws.name: Dark Secret
merits_flaws.description: Possesses Obtenebration, a Sabbat discipline strongly associated with Lasombra. If this becomes widely known, many would assume he's a Sabbat plant or corrupted. Must keep this power hidden.

merits_flaws.name: Nightmares
merits_flaws.description: Relives being buried alive, clawing through dirt, the desert walk. Sometimes wakes during day in panic. -1 die to all actions for the night after particularly bad episodes (ST discretion).

merits_flaws.name: Prey Exclusion
merits_flaws.description: Cannot feed from business executives, corporate managers, or 'successful' businesspeople - his former peers. Something psychological about his fall from his old life. Must feed from working-class people: truck drivers, diner staff, gas station attendants, etc.
